setup.title: Welcome to NixOS Configurator
setup.intro:
Where is your NixOS configuration directory?
NiCo additionally stores a nico.json there with its settings.
dirbrowser.title: Select directory
setup.confirmSuffix: does not exist yet. Should NiCo create it?
setup.hwCopied: hardware-configuration.nix copied from /etc/nixos/ and added as import.
setup.hwPresent: hardware-configuration.nix found and added as import.
setup.symlinkQuestion: Should /etc/nixos be set up as a symlink to this directory?
setup.symlinkHint: If /etc/nixos points to this directory, nixos-rebuild will automatically use this configuration.
header.quitTitle: Stop NiCo server – you can close the tab afterwards
field.userPassHint: (stored in plain text in the config)
field.shellHint: zsh and fish: NiCo automatically adds programs.zsh/fish.enable = true.
field.avahiHint: (network service discovery, e.g. for network printers)
btn.saveTitle: Saves current settings in NiCo – no changes to your NixOS config
btn.adminTitle: Open admin panel: write, export, syntax check, Git rollback
btn.brixInsertTitle: Insert a new Nix brick into the config – NiCo never modifies Nix bricks
btn.brixMoveTitle: Move an existing Nix brick to another position
pkg.searching: Searching … (may take a moment the first time)
rebuild.restartHint: A restart may be required to apply all changes.
dryrun.title: Syntax check
dryrun.networkError: Network error – is the server reachable?
brix.insertInfo: Nix bricks are manual Nix sections that NiCo never modifies. NiCo automatically assigns a unique ID.
brix.errInvalidName: Name may only contain letters, numbers, and hyphens.
brix.deleteBtnTitle: Delete Nix brick "{0}" – content will be lost
brix.confirmDelete:
Delete Nix brick "{0}"?

The content will be lost and cannot be recovered.
brix.moveInfo: Select the target section and desired position (1 = top).
brix.movePosError: Please enter a valid position (1 or higher).
brix.renameInfo: Only the label changes – the numeric ID stays the same.
brix.splitInfo: After which line should the brick be split? (1 = after the first content line)
brix.errSplitLine: Please enter a valid line number (1 or higher).
write.title: Write configuration
write.info:
NiCo writes the current configuration to your NixOS directory and creates a checkpoint – like a time machine for your config.

You can return to any earlier checkpoint in the admin panel if something goes wrong.
write.labelPlaceholder: e.g. "Before KDE switch" or "First working config"
admin.title: Admin panel
admin.settings.hmDirHint: Subdirectory in the config folder containing Home Manager .nix files (default: home)
admin.saveTitle: Saves current settings in NiCo – no changes to your NixOS config
admin.dryrunTitle: Checks the Nix syntax of the generated configuration.nix – read-only
admin.exportTitle: Creates a ZIP file with all config files for backup or transfer
admin.writeTitle: Writes the config directly to your NixOS directory – changes take effect immediately
admin.checkpointTitle: Saves the current state and creates a point in the time machine you can return to at any time
admin.rebuildTitle: Runs nixos-rebuild switch – writes config first, requires root (sudo)
rebuild.title: NixOS Rebuild
admin.gitHint: NiCo automatically creates a Git commit after every write. "Reset" restores the Nix files to the selected state – the Git history is fully preserved.
admin.rollbackBtnTitle: Restore Nix files to this state (new commit – history preserved)
git.notInstalled: ⚠ Git is not installed – strongly recommended (time machine). Please install Git and restart NiCo.
git.noRepo: ⚠ No Git repository – time machine not active. Create one now?
git.initConfirm:
NiCo can create a local Git repository to track changes.

Create it now?
sudo.title: Sudo password
sudo.hint: Enter your sudo password to perform system operations.
git.rollbackDetail:
The Nix files will be restored. A new commit will be created –
the Git history will be fully preserved.
git.remoteBehind: Local repository is {0} commit(s) behind the remote.
git.startGuardBehindBody: The local repository is {0} commit(s) behind the remote. If NiCo loads now, it will work with an outdated local state.
git.startGuardDivergedTitle: Local and remote state have diverged
git.startGuardDivergedBody: The local repository is {0} commit(s) ahead and {1} commit(s) behind the remote. Loading in NiCo now can easily lead to processing the wrong state.
git.startGuardDirtyBody: The local repository has uncommitted changes. NiCo would work with this local intermediate state.
git.startGuardErrorBody: The Git status could not be cleanly checked before the first load: {0}
git.startGuardSyncRecommendation: Recommendation: sync outside NiCo first (e.g. git pull), then reopen.
git.startGuardDirtyRecommendation: Recommendation: review or commit first, so NiCo does not work on an unintended intermediate state.
git.startGuardErrorRecommendation: Recommendation: check Git status manually first. You can still open the config locally, but NiCo will load without a reliable remote reference.
git.startGuardDivergedRecommendation: The repository must be cleaned up outside NiCo first (e.g. git rebase or git merge in the terminal). NiCo cannot work safely in this state.
git.guard.s2.fetchDesc: The local state will be completely replaced by the remote state. Local commits and changes will be permanently lost.
git.guard.s2.sendDesc: The local state will be forcefully written to the remote. Remote changes will be permanently lost.
git.guard.status.localNew: Local has {0} commit(s) missing on remote
git.aborted.info:
NiCo was opened without a loaded config.
A config can be reconfigured via the NixOS menu.
git.closePrompt.title: Local changes not yet pushed
git.closePrompt.body: There are local commits or uncommitted changes that are not yet online. Push now before NiCo closes?
quit.done: NiCo has been stopped. You can close this tab.
about.title: About NiCo
about.author: Created by
about.thirdPartyVal: Prism.js for syntax highlighting (MIT) · see THIRD_PARTY_LICENSES.md
help.title: Help & Documentation
errors.ERR_SYSTEM_PATH: Access to system directory not allowed.
errors.ERR_NOT_SETUP: NiCo is not set up yet.
errors.ERR_NOT_A_DIR: The path is not a directory.
errors.ERR_DRY_NO_NIX: nix-instantiate not found. Please start NiCo in a NixOS environment.
errors.ERR_DRY_TIMEOUT_SEM: Timeout during semantic validation (60 seconds). Note: The semantic check takes longer on first run.
tools.title: Tools
panel.nd.name: Unrecognized File
panel.nd.info: This file is not recognized by NiCo. Changes in the code window are saved immediately – proceed with caution.

panel.hw.info: hardware-configuration.nix is automatically generated by NixOS. Manual changes can destabilize the system.

panel.hm.name: Home Manager
panel.unknown.name: Unknown type
setType.title: Set file type
setType.hint: This file has no NiCo type marker yet. What kind of file is this?
sidebar.title: Files
sidebar.saveAllTitle: Save all open files at once
sidebar.unsavedWarning: This file has unsaved changes. Close anyway?
sidebar.maxTabs: Maximum 8 tabs open. Please close a tab first.
errors.ERR_IMPORT_PERMISSION: Permission denied. /etc/nixos is owned by root – NiCo running as a regular user cannot read these files. Please use manual import.
errors.ERR_DETACH_FAILED: The config could not be detached from NiCo.
errors.ERR_MANAGED_FILE: This file is managed by NiCo.
errors.ERR_BRICK_NAME_EXISTS: A Nix-Brick with this name already exists.
errors.ERR_INVALID_NAME: Invalid name – only letters, numbers, and hyphens allowed.
unsaved.title: Unsaved Changes
unsaved.message: You have unsaved changes. What should happen before switching files?
import.title: Import existing configuration?
import.intro: NiCo found a NixOS configuration. Should NiCo import the known settings?
import.introWithPath: NiCo found a NixOS configuration ({0}). Should NiCo import the known settings?
import.introNoSource: No NixOS configuration found in the configured directory. Import now or search manually?
import.brixInfo: Unknown sections saved as Nix brick 1-imported-config.
import.backupConfirm: The target directory is not empty. All files will be backed up as "{0}". Continue?
admin.symlinkHint: A symlink makes /etc/nixos point to your NiCo directory. Then nixos-rebuild switch works without specifying a path. The original is saved as /etc/nixos.bak and can be restored at any time.
admin.symlinkActive: ✓ /etc/nixos already points to this NiCo directory ({0})
admin.symlinkOther: ⚠ /etc/nixos is a symlink to a different directory: {0}
admin.symlinkIsDir: /etc/nixos is a regular directory – symlink can be created.
errors.ERR_SYMLINK_OTHER_TARGET: Symlink already points to a different directory.
errors.ERR_BACKUP_EXISTS: /etc/nixos.bak already exists – please check manually first.
errors.ERR_NO_BACKUP: /etc/nixos.bak not found – no backup to restore.
admin.settingsExportTitle: Back up program settings (language, theme, view options - without config path)
admin.settingsImportConfirm: Apply settings from file? The config path stays unchanged.
admin.detachHint: Creates a ZIP backup, removes NiCo comment lines from all .nix files, and deletes the NiCo metadata file of the config. Your own files stay in place.
admin.detachWarn: After that, no config path remains stored in NiCo and the startup dialog appears again.
admin.detachConfirm:
Detach the active config from NiCo?

A ZIP backup will be created first. Then NiCo only removes its own comment and marker lines from all .nix files, deletes config.json or nico.json, and forgets the config path.
admin.detachSuccess: Config detached from NiCo. Backup created: {0}
admin.zipConfirmText: {0} found. All existing files will be backed up and replaced. Import?
admin.zipInvalidHint: This ZIP does not contain a configuration.nix or flake.nix at the top level.
admin.importHint: Read an existing NixOS configuration. Known fields are imported, the rest is saved as a Nix brick.
admin.importPathHint: Select a directory or enter a path. NiCo looks for configuration.nix inside it.
errors.ERR_IMPORT_PERMISSION: No read permission for this file.
errors.ERR_WRITE_NO_GIT: No Git repository found. Please create one first (Admin panel → Git).
hm.enableHint: Generates a home.nix – username and stateVersion are taken from the system settings.
hm.shellInitExtraHint: Appended to the shell init file.
preview.modeRaw: Simple editor – no panel available yet
preview.plainInactive: Annotated view active – click for plain (without Brix markers)
preview.plainActive: Plain view active (without Brix markers) – click for annotated view
admin.settings.codeViewPlain: Code view on start: plain (without Brix annotations)
preview.flakeDisabled: Flakes are disabled. Enable them under Nix & System.
preview.hmDisabled: Home Manager is disabled. Enable it under User.
sidebar.rawPanelHint: This file is not managed by NiCo. You can edit and save it on the right.
sidebar.hwWarnText: hardware-configuration.nix is generated automatically by NixOS. Manual changes may destabilize the system.
sidebar.hwSaveConfirm:
hardware-configuration.nix is generated by NixOS – manual changes may destabilize the system.

Save anyway?
sidebar.hwImportInfo: Choose an existing hardware-configuration.nix to copy into {dir}.
sidebar.hwImportAlreadyListed: This file is already listed or is inside the current config.
sidebar.hwImportEmptyList: No matches yet. Optionally check a path manually.
sidebar.hwImportSuccessBackup: Existing hardware-configuration.nix was backed up as .bak and the new file was imported.
git.writeWithoutGit:
No Git repository active – changes cannot be undone.

Continue anyway?
hm.useGlobalPkgs: useGlobalPkgs – use system packages for Home Manager
hm.useUserPackages: useUserPackages – allow package installation via nix-env
admin.settings.nicoHint: Program settings – stay on this machine.
admin.settings.configHint: These settings belong to the configuration and travel when copied.
admin.settings.hmDirHint: Subdirectory for Home Manager files (default: home)
admin.sections.settingsHint: Choose which sections should be shown by default. Sections with content are always shown regardless of this setting.
admin.validation.title: Validation
admin.validation.hint: Defines which static checks run before rebuild or dry run. This setting travels with the config.
admin.validation.settingsHint: Active rules are checked on every validation. Flake rules only appear for flake configs.
host.label: Configuration:
host.switchInfo: The main configuration (Defaults) should be saved before editing a host.
file.hwWarning: Warning: hardware-configuration.nix is normally generated by NixOS. Manual changes may be overwritten by the next nixos-generate-config.
fl.hosts.brixNote: Outputs are managed by a Brix block.
fl.hosts.managedNote: NiCo manages nixosConfigurations. Arch & specialArgs apply to all hosts.
fl.hosts.errExists: A host with this name already exists.
